prompt: I pushed my Jekyll blog to GitHub Pages and the Actions build is failing with `Could not find gem 'jekyll-paginate-v2'`. My Gemfile has both `github-pages` and `jekyll-paginate-v2` in `:jekyll_plugins`. It builds fine locally with `bundle exec jekyll serve`. Repo is at github.com/me/blog and the site lives at me.github.io/blog. What do I need to change?

assertions.text: Identifies that jekyll-paginate-v2 is NOT on the GitHub Pages plugin allowlist as the root cause
Explains why the same Gemfile works locally but fails on the GitHub Pages classic builder
Offers migrating to GitHub Actions deployment as a fix (unlocks current Jekyll + any plugin)
Offers an in-allowlist alternative (e.g., drop jekyll-paginate-v2 and use jekyll-paginate)
Acknowledges the site lives at /blog (project site) — does not assume root deployment
Does not (incorrectly) attribute the failure solely to bundle install / Gemfile.lock issues
